author: Tertullian

title: Concerning the Plea of “Pleasing the Husband.”

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: As if I were speaking to Gentiles, addressing you with a Gentile precept, and (one which is) common to all, (I would say,) “You are bound to please your husbands only.”

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: But you will please them in proportion as you take no care to please others.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Be ye without carefulness, blessed (sisters): no wife is “ugly” to her own husband.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: She “pleased” him enough when she was selected (by him as his wife); whether commended by form or by character.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Let none of you think that, if she abstain from the care of her person, she will incur the hatred and aversion of husbands.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Every husband is the exactor of chastity; but beauty, a believing (husband) does not require, because we are not captivated by the same graces which the Gentiles think (to be) graces: an unbelieving one, on the other hand, even regards with suspicion, just from that infamous opinion of us which the Gentiles have.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: For whom, then, is it that you cherish your beauty?

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: If for a believer, he does not exact it: if for an unbeliever, he does not believe in it unless it be artless.

verses.book: On the Apparel of Women - Book II
verses.text: Why are you eager to please either one who is suspicious, or else one who desires it not?
